paragraphs.en: Presidential politics are about two things. It's about money and votes. And he's working on both right now. He wants to get out and try to make himself be a victim. We've seen politicians, some of which may be in the White House, always want to be the victim of press or whatever investigation may be happening. And Newsom is positioning himself politically to do that. And as, Sabrina, a consultant, it's a chef's kiss is what this is.
paragraphs.cn: Presidential politics is about two things: money and votes. He's working on both now. He wants to step forward and try to portray himself as a victim. We've seen some politicians, possibly including the one in the White House, always want to be victims of the media or any investigation. Newsom is positioning himself politically. As an advisor, Sabrina, this is a perfect move.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: A chef's kiss of getting ahead of it on camera with the flags behind him. I'm surprised he hasn't run away to another country saying, I'm going to go into exile until Donald Trump leaves me alone and tweet it out.
paragraphs.cn: Getting ahead of the cameras with flags behind him, this is a perfect move. I'm surprised he didn't run to another country and say, I'm going into exile until Donald Trump stops bothering me, and then tweet.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, let's -- let's let people know, back in May, Emerson College actually did do a poll looking at people's potential choices, Democratic nominee for president. And Newsom was not the first name on it.
paragraphs.cn: Okay, let's tell everyone that back in May, Emerson College did a poll asking about potential presidential candidates, Democratic nomination. Newsom wasn't the first name on the list.

paragraphs.speaker: Rodriguez
paragraphs.en: I mean, there's no question that Newsom is amongst the top contenders. He does come up as one of the --
paragraphs.cn: I mean, no doubt Newsom is one of the top contenders. He really is one of them --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. I mean, I'm amongst the top contenders. Like, that list is so long at this point.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. I mean, I'm also one of the top contenders. Like, the list is too long now.

paragraphs.speaker: Rodriguez
paragraphs.en: But, no. But he is certainly one of -- he has -- he has established himself under Trump's second term as like one of the leading people trying to take him on.
paragraphs.cn: But, no. He really is one of them -- he has already established himself as one of the main figures trying to challenge Trump in his second term.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. We'll post a sample tweet so people can see.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. We'll post a sample tweet so everyone can see.

paragraphs.speaker: Rodriguez
paragraphs.en: So, this furthers -- so this furthers that national profile that he's tried to build. I mean he has specifically, strategically, over the last year and a half, tried to take on President Trump consistently.
paragraphs.cn: So, this further -- this further raises his national profile, which he has been working on. I mean, over the past year and a half, he has been strategically and consistently trying to challenge President Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Prop 50 (ph). Prop 50 (ph).
paragraphs.cn: Proposition 50. Proposition 50.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, redistricting. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, redistricting. Yes.

paragraphs.speaker: Rodriguez
paragraphs.en: Whether it's with redistricting, whether it was last year with the National Guard deployment in Los Angeles, he has, at every turn, tried to take on President Trump. And this does further that.
paragraphs.cn: Whether it's redistricting or deploying the National Guard in Los Angeles last year, he has tried to challenge President Trump at every turn. And this furthers that.

paragraphs.speaker: Rodriguez
paragraphs.en: And we do see it. I mean, he is already fundraising off of it and sending emails. So, it's clearly a position.
paragraphs.cn: We did see that. I mean, he has already started fundraising and sending emails from this. So, this is clearly a stance.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. And we just showed that tweet, not because we think it's funny, but because, to your point, Newsom likes to, quote/unquote, do online battle with the Trump administration in the language that they, quote/unquote, understand, so to speak, the language of memes and jokes and he's been at the forefront of that.
paragraphs.cn: Exactly. We show that tweet, not because we find it funny, but because, as you said, Newsom likes to use what they call 'the language of the know' -- that is, the language of memes and jokes -- to engage in 'online battles' with the Trump administration, and he has been at the forefront of this.

paragraphs.speaker: Fischer
paragraphs.en: And all of those contenders that you just put up on that list, they're all following in his footsteps and launching podcasts and doing their own direct to consumer media and messaging. That's what this is all a big part of. Gavin Newsom doesn't want anyone to be reaching his potential voters except for him.
paragraphs.cn: And the contenders you just listed are all following his lead, launching podcasts and doing direct-to-consumer media and messaging. That's a big part of this. Gavin Newsom doesn't want anyone else to reach his potential voters.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, Sara Fischer, thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: Okay, Sara Fischer, thank you so much for coming.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can't wait to have you back. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to talk more, a little bit, about 2028. We've got J.D. Vance reflecting as he sets up a potential bid. J.D. VANCE, VICE PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES (July 2021): We're effectively run in this country via the Democrats, via our corporate oligarchs, by a bunch of childless cat ladies.
paragraphs.cn: Looking forward to having you again. Next on CNN This Morning, we'll discuss the 2028 election further. J.D. Vance reflected as he prepares for a possible campaign. U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance (July 2021): 'Our country is actually run by Democrats, by our corporate oligarchs, by a bunch of childless cat ladies.'

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, that was back in 2021. We're going to talk about why Vance is now saying that that moment was one of the dumbest things he's ever said. J.D. VANCE, VICE PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES (February 28, 2025): I recognize very much that I am a baby Catholic. That there are things about the faith that I don't know. If you ever hear me pontificating about the Catholic faith, please recognize, it comes from a place of deep belief, but it also comes from a place of not always knowing everything all the time.
paragraphs.cn: That was 2021. We'll discuss why Vance now says that was one of the dumbest things he ever said. U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance (February 28, 2025): 'I am very aware that I am a Catholic novice. There are many things I don't know about faith. If you hear me pontificating about the Catholic faith, remember that it comes from deep faith, but also from not always knowing everything.'

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that was just in 2025, I think, at the National Prayer Breakfast. Vice President J.D. Vance admitting, look, he's new to the Catholic Church. At the same time, he has also made it a central part of his political identity. It's the main topic of his new book. And CNN obtained an early copy. It's out today. It's called "Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith." In it Vance talks about his conversion to Catholicism, but also his conversion from Trump critic to Trump's vice president.
paragraphs.cn: Okay, that was in 2025, I think, at the National Prayer Breakfast. Vice President J.D. Vance acknowledged, you see, he is a new member of the Catholic Church. At the same time, he makes it a core part of his political identity. This is a major topic of his new book. CNN obtained an early copy. Published today. The title is 'Communion: Finding the Way Back to Faith.' In the book, Vance talks about his conversion to Catholicism and his journey from Trump critic to Trump's vice president.

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: There's this sense that God was always looking out for me. Even when I strayed from the path, even when I called myself an atheist, that faith that my grandmother had sort of imbued me with was always there in the background and, of course, is an important part of the way that I go about my duties today. But it's just an important part of who I am. It fundamentally illuminates everything else about me.
paragraphs.cn: Ada perasaan bahwa Tuhan selalu memberkati saya. Bahkan ketika saya menyimpang dari jalan, bahkan ketika saya menyebut diri saya ateis, keyakinan yang ditanamkan nenek saya selalu ada di latar belakang, dan tentu saja, itu adalah bagian penting dari cara saya menjalankan tugas hari ini. Tapi itu hanya bagian penting dari identitas saya. Itu pada dasarnya menerangi segalanya tentang diri saya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining us now in the group chat, Christopher Hale, publisher of "The Letters from Leo" Substack. We've been talking to you a lot because every time Vance or the Trump administration has gotten sideways with Pope Leo, we call you up and we're like, what's -- what gives? What about this book strikes you about how he talks about his Catholicism and how it informs how he thinks about governing? CHRISTOPHER HALE, PUBLISHER, "ETTERS FROM LEO"
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang bergabung dengan kami dalam obrolan grup adalah Christopher Hale, penerbit Substack 'Letters from Leo'. Kami sering berbicara dengan Anda karena setiap kali pemerintahan Vance atau Trump bentrok dengan Paus Leo, kami menelepon Anda dan bertanya: Ada apa? Dalam buku ini, dia berbicara tentang iman Katoliknya dan bagaimana hal itu memengaruhi filosofi pemerintahannya. Apa yang paling berkesan bagi Anda? Christopher Hale, penerbit 'Letters from Leo'.

paragraphs.speaker: Substack
paragraphs.en: You know, I think the first thing that I think is remarkable is not -- what's not in the book. I mean, so that speech he talked about where he said he had made some mistakes. Really, the first week of his administration, he said two things about the Catholic Church. He accused the Catholic bishops of getting rich off migrants' resettlement in the United States and he --
paragraphs.cn: Anda tahu, saya pikir hal pertama yang mencolok adalah -- -- bukan apa yang tertulis di buku. Maksud saya, dalam pidato yang dia sebutkan, dia mengatakan dia membuat beberapa kesalahan. Sebenarnya, pada minggu pertama menjabat, dia mengatakan dua hal tentang Gereja Katolik. Dia menuduh para uskup Katolik menjadi kaya dengan memukimkan kembali imigran di Amerika, dan dia -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Which I think he actually ended up apologizing for.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir dia kemudian meminta maaf untuk itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: He apologized the Cardinal Dolan for it. And then he said that he gave his theological expose on ordo amoris, a good Latin phrase from Saint Augustine about the order of love. And he got smacked down. One of the last acts that Pope Francis did was write this letter to the bishops of the United States talking about what it means to have an order of love. That letter was actually ghostwritten by an American cardinal named Robert Prvost, who became Pope Leo XIV. These weren't in the book.
paragraphs.cn: Dia meminta maaf kepada Kardinal Dolan. Lalu dia mengatakan dia memberikan penjelasan teologis tentang ordo amoris, sebuah frase Latin yang bagus dari St. Agustinus tentang tatanan cinta. Ternyata dia mendapat sanggahan. Salah satu tindakan terakhir Paus Fransiskus adalah menulis surat kepada para uskup Amerika tentang apa arti tatanan cinta. Surat itu sebenarnya ditulis oleh seorang kardinal Amerika bernama Robert Prevost, yang kemudian menjadi Paus Leo XIV. Semua ini tidak ada di buku.

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: And I think that's -- I think that speaks a lot that he would --
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir itu sangat jelas, dia -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Meaning, he didn't do a story of, like, here's what I learned when I actually dealt with popes and when I talked publicly. It's about his personal, past journey.
paragraphs.cn: Artinya, dia tidak menceritakan kisah seperti "Apa yang saya pelajari ketika benar-benar berurusan dengan Paus dan berbicara di depan umum." Buku ini tentang perjalanan pribadinya di masa lalu.

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. And I think that for Catholics who he -- of course, he converted to Catholicism in 2019. He's the only Catholic I know who's gotten rebuked by two popes in one year.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu. Dan saya pikir, baginya -- -- tentu saja, dia masuk Katolik pada tahun 2019. Dia satu-satunya Katolik yang saya kenal yang ditegur oleh dua Paus dalam satu tahun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, he's just the most high profile. There's also the zeal of the convert, right?
paragraphs.cn: Yah, dia hanya yang paling menonjol. Dan ada semangat seorang mualaf, kan?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He brings that energy.
paragraphs.cn: Dia membawa energi itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: And, you know, the church loves converts. We have -- we have places for converts. But like any, any tradition, when the new guy comes in and tells you you're doing things wrong, there can be issues there.
paragraphs.cn: Dan, Anda tahu, gereja menyukai mualaf. Kami memiliki -- -- kami memiliki tempat untuk mualaf. Tapi seperti tradisi lainnya, ketika pendatang baru memberi tahu Anda bahwa Anda melakukan sesuatu yang salah, itu bisa menimbulkan masalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. One thing about getting things wrong, because I doubled checked this three different ways. Like, I'm not sure this is true. That the church on the cover of this book is not a Catholic Church. And it's worth mentioning. The publisher actually issued a statement to "USA Today" saying that this church comes from the part of the country where Vice President Vance grew up.
paragraphs.cn: Oke. Tentang poin melakukan kesalahan, karena saya sudah memverifikasinya dengan tiga cara berbeda. Saya tidak yakin itu benar. Gereja di sampul buku ini bukan gereja Katolik. Ini perlu disebutkan. Penerbit sebenarnya mengeluarkan pernyataan kepada USA Today bahwa gereja ini berasal dari daerah tempat Wakil Presiden Vance dibesarkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: "He was raised protestant and in evangelical churches, and he talks about Christianity in an ecumenical sense throughout the book." Penny, I think you said you're familiar with this area.
paragraphs.cn: "Dia dibesarkan di gereja Protestan dan Evangelikal, dan dia berbicara tentang Kekristenan dari sudut pandang gereja universal di seluruh buku." Penny, saya pikir Anda mengatakan Anda akrab dengan daerah itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Nance
paragraphs.en: Yes. Yes, I actually grew up --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Ya, saya sebenarnya dibesarkan di sana -- --

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Cornish
paragraphs.en: But would it -- I may -- I'm from Massachusetts. I feel like you kick a rock, you hit a Catholic Church. Was there not one?
paragraphs.cn: Tapi -- -- saya mungkin -- -- saya dari Massachusetts. Saya rasa Anda bisa menendang batu dan menemukan gereja Katolik. Tidak ada di sana?

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Nance
paragraphs.en: No. I mean, you know, I'm proud -- I'm a proud hillbilly. And I'm not actually at all surprised that he wrote this book. And if you understand where he grew up, you understand that he was very impacted by the faith of his grandmother.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak. Maksud saya, Anda tahu, saya bangga -- -- saya bangga menjadi orang gunung. Saya sama sekali tidak terkejut dia menulis buku ini. Jika Anda tahu tempat dia dibesarkan, Anda akan mengerti bahwa dia sangat dipengaruhi oleh iman neneknya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, this was in "Hillbilly Elegy" as well.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, itu juga disebutkan di 'Hillbilly Elegy'.

paragraphs.speaker: Nance
paragraphs.en: Who was a Evangelical Christian. And there's not many Catholics in Kentucky in the area he grew up. In Ohio, it tends to be a much more evangelical area. But people of deep faith. And despite what he went through in college and all the trials that he lived through as a child, his mother's drug use, faith was always there. He departed for it briefly -- from it briefly, but came back.
paragraphs.cn: Dia adalah seorang Kristen Evangelikal. Di daerah Kentucky tempat dia dibesarkan, tidak banyak Katolik. Di Ohio, itu lebih merupakan daerah Evangelikal. Tapi orang-orang di sana memiliki iman yang dalam. Terlepas dari semua yang dia alami di perguruan tinggi, dan kesulitan masa kecilnya, masalah narkoba ibunya, iman selalu ada. Dia sempat pergi -- -- sempat pergi, tapi kembali.

paragraphs.speaker: Nance
paragraphs.en: And what this book is about, I think is about how he got back to his faith.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir buku ini tentang bagaimana dia kembali pada iman.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And that story feels familiar for those of us who grew up in the George Bush era as well, of kind of like church can bring you. I want to talk about one other thing, though, in this book about communion. He talks about a comment he made on Fox News five years ago. Let me play it for you.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Dan cerita ini juga akrab bagi kita yang tumbuh di era George W. Bush, seperti apa yang bisa diberikan gereja. Tapi, saya ingin membicarakan hal lain dalam buku ini tentang Ekaristi. Dia menyebutkan sebuah pernyataan yang dia buat di Fox News lima tahun lalu. Biarkan saya memutarnya untuk Anda.

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.en: We're effectively run in this country via the Democrats, via our corporate oligarchs, by a bunch of childless cat ladies who are miserable at their own lives and the choices that they've made, and so they want to make the rest of the country miserable too.
paragraphs.cn: Negara ini sebenarnya dijalankan oleh Demokrat, oligarki perusahaan, dan sekelompok wanita kucing tanpa anak yang menderita karena hidup dan pilihan mereka, jadi mereka ingin orang lain di negara ini juga menderita.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He doesn't need to, in this very personal book called "Communion," go to a political fumble. Why do you think he did?
paragraphs.cn: Dalam buku yang sangat pribadi berjudul 'Ekaristi' ini, dia tidak perlu melakukan kesalahan politik. Menurut Anda mengapa dia melakukannya?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Because he wants to run for president, that's why.
paragraphs.cn: Karena dia ingin mencalonkan diri sebagai presiden, itu alasannya.

paragraphs.speaker: B -- Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because he -- no, but he called it one of the dumbest things I ever said, boneheaded.
paragraphs.cn: Karena dia -- -- tidak, tapi dia menyebut itu salah satu hal paling bodoh yang pernah saya katakan, sangat bodoh.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: But he's also -- he's also, look, I'm not going to sit here and defend J.D. Vance. The only thing we got in common is we both had a mamaw (ph). But the reason he wrote the book, he wants to run for president, and he's trying to get in touch with a very important part of the electorate. I'm not saying that he's not Christian or not theological or all the things out there, but he's trying to become a real person and have a little bit of a redemption story.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi dia juga -- -- dia juga, dengar, saya tidak akan duduk di sini membela J.D. Vance. Satu-satunya kesamaan kami adalah kami sama-sama punya nenek. Tapi alasan dia menulis buku ini adalah karena dia ingin mencalonkan diri sebagai presiden, dan dia mencoba terhubung dengan kelompok pemilih yang sangat penting. Saya tidak mengatakan dia tidak percaya Kristen atau tidak punya konsep teologi, tapi dia berusaha menjadi orang yang autentik dan memiliki kisah penebusan.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: And as somebody who loves a redemption story, because I am a redemption story, I can respect that. And the church is in Tennessee, just
paragraphs.cn: Sebagai seseorang yang mencintai kisah penebusan, karena saya sendiri adalah kisah penebusan, saya bisa menghargai itu. Dan gerejanya di Tennessee, hanya

paragraphs.speaker: Fyi. Cornish
paragraphs.en: Wait, which, the picture of the church? .
paragraphs.cn: Tunggu, yang mana, foto gerejanya?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Not -- Virginia. The one on the -- on the book was in -- it's in -- yes, it's in Virginia.
paragraphs.cn: Bukan -- -- Virginia. Yang di buku itu di -- -- di -- -- ya, di Virginia.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: On the cover. No, no, we've got to -- we've got to verify. Oh, yes, Elk Creek, Virginia. There you go. Mount Zion UMC, shout out to y'all because you got a bunch of attention you didn't think you were going to get.
paragraphs.cn: Di sampulnya. Tidak, tidak, kita harus -- -- kita harus memeriksanya. Oh, ya, Elk Creek, Virginia. Itu dia. Gereja Methodist United Mount Zion, salut untuk kalian karena kalian mendapat banyak perhatian yang tidak kalian duga.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Yes. And it's lovely.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, dan itu lucu.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. It's a beautiful backdrop.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu, latar belakangnya indah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We do group chats, and you're new, so what's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: Kami punya grup chat, kamu baru, apa yang ada di grup chatmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: So, keep me on the same beat with the pope. Spike Lee, last November, saw Pope Leo XIV and he asked for a miracle. He wanted the Knicks to win their first NBA championship since 1973. And he did.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, biarkan saya mengikuti ritme Paus. November lalu, Spike Lee bertemu Paus Leo XIV, dan dia meminta sebuah keajaiban. Dia berharap Knicks memenangkan kejuaraan NBA untuk pertama kalinya sejak 1973. Dan dia melakukannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: And so he was paraded through Fort Greene, Spike Lee, on -- after game four. They were down 29 points and they came back in victory. And if you look at him on his little vehicle, he kind of looks like the pope at Fort Greene.
paragraphs.cn: Maka, setelah pertandingan keempat, Spike Lee diarak dalam perayaan di Fort Greene. Mereka sempat tertinggal 29 poin, tapi akhirnya berbalik menang. Jika kamu melihat dia duduk di mobil kecil itu, dia terlihat sedikit seperti Paus dari Fort Greene.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I was about to say, why is Spike Lee in a Popemobile? Like, what happened? There's like a Knicks pope. He's -- his little head's popping out of the car.
paragraphs.cn: Saya baru saja mau bilang, kenapa Spike Lee duduk di mobil Paus? Apa yang terjadi? Seperti ada Paus Knicks. Kepala kecilnya menjulur dari mobil.

paragraphs.speaker: Hale
paragraphs.en: I -- he saw Leo's Popemobile and he wanted the same thing.
paragraphs.cn: Saya -- -- dia melihat mobil Paus Leo, dan dia menginginkan hal yang sama.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, that's another spike in popularity for the pope if he's responsible for this. He's going to have both Chicago and New York in his corner. Can I ask you, Penny, because you haven't been here for a while, what's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, jika Paus yang mewujudkannya, popularitasnya naik lagi. Chicago dan New York akan mendukungnya. Boleh saya tanya, Penny, karena kamu sudah lama tidak datang, apa yang ada di grup chatmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Nance
paragraphs.en: I would like to remind everybody about HHS' new program, moms.gov. It's a great place for women who are needing help to find out where the local pregnancy care centers are, what services are available for them, and just -- it's called moms.gov. It's all indexed by your zip code. So, we'll just say, if you know someone who needs help, that's a great place to go.
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin mengingatkan semua orang tentang proyek baru dari Departemen Kesehatan dan Layanan Masyarakat, moms.gov. Ini situs yang bagus, wanita yang membutuhkan bantuan bisa menemukan pusat perawatan kehamilan lokal, dan mengetahui layanan apa yang tersedia. Namanya moms.gov, diindeks berdasarkan kode pos. Jadi, jika kamu kenal seseorang yang butuh bantuan, itu tempat yang bagus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And thy holy political consultant, what say you?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, penasihat politik yang suci, bagaimana menurutmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Today in Washington D.C., is election day.
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini adalah hari pemilihan di Washington, D.C.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: Go vote if you can get out and go vote in election day today in
paragraphs.cn: Jika kamu bisa keluar, pergilah memilih, hari ini adalah hari pemilihan.

paragraphs.speaker: Rocha
paragraphs.en: And stop sending me mail. Today is -- the election day is over. I don't need any more mail. I voted four weeks ago.
paragraphs.cn: Berhenti mengirimi saya surat. Hari ini -- -- hari pemilihan sudah selesai. Saya tidak perlu surat lagi. Saya sudah memilih empat minggu lalu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. The good reminder, there are still these elections going on, and we'll soon be talking about a new mayor of D.C. In the meantime, thank you for being here. Thanks for waking up with us. We're going to have the headlines coming up next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, sebagai pengingat, pemilihan masih berlangsung, dan kami akan segera membahas walikota baru Washington, D.C. Sementara itu, terima kasih telah menonton, terima kasih sudah bangun pagi bersama kami. Selanjutnya, berita utama.

vocabulary.en: to bring to an end
vocabulary.excerpt: if I didn't terminate that deal, I believe that they would have -- Israel wouldn't be here right now.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Jika saya tidak mengakhiri perjanjian itu, saya yakin mereka -- -- Israel tidak akan ada sekarang.

vocabulary.cn: 核尘埃（特朗普对核设施被摧毁后的比喻）
vocabulary.en: a term used by Trump to describe the remnants of a destroyed nuclear facility
vocabulary.excerpt: the second one was what we did with respect to the nuclear dust, as I call it.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 第二件事就是我们针对核尘埃所做的，我这么称呼它。

vocabulary.en: to destroy something by explosion
vocabulary.excerpt: We blew it up. The mountain collapsed on top.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们把它炸毁了。山体塌陷压在上面。

vocabulary.en: to point a weapon at; to direct something towards
vocabulary.excerpt: he had all those missiles aimed at the emir, at Saudi Arabia, at UAE, at Kuwait, at Bahrain.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他把所有导弹都瞄准了埃米尔、沙特阿拉伯、阿联酋、科威特、巴林。

vocabulary.en: not logical or reasonable
vocabulary.excerpt: They're totally irrational people.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们完全是些不理性的人。

vocabulary.word: blow off the face of the Earth
vocabulary.phonetic: /bloʊ ɔːf ðə feɪs əv ði ɜːrθ/
vocabulary.cn: 从地球上抹去（彻底摧毁）
vocabulary.en: to destroy completely
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel would have been blown off the face of the Earth, 100 percent.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Israel sudah lama dihapus dari muka bumi, seratus persen.

vocabulary.en: a document outlining the terms of an agreement between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: the president speak about this proposed memorandum of agreement with Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Presiden berbicara tentang nota kesepahaman yang diusulkan dengan Iran.

vocabulary.en: the top level of leadership
vocabulary.excerpt: the first tier of leadership was gone after strikes
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kepemimpinan eselon pertama menghilang setelah serangan.

vocabulary.en: the second level of leadership
vocabulary.excerpt: the second tier of leadership was gone after U.S. strikes.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kepemimpinan eselon kedua menghilang setelah serangan AS.

vocabulary.en: to be persistently troubled by something from the past
vocabulary.excerpt: the president is still haunted by the shadow of Barack Obama's nuclear deal.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Presiden masih dihantui bayang-bayang kesepakatan nuklir Barack Obama.

vocabulary.en: to think about something constantly
vocabulary.excerpt: he spent most of the time obsessing over how he did a better deal than Obama
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia menghabiskan sebagian besar waktunya terobsesi dengan bagaimana dia bisa lebih baik dari Obama.

vocabulary.cn: Pemimpin Tertinggi (pemimpin politik dan agama tertinggi Iran)
vocabulary.en: the highest-ranking political and religious leader in Iran
vocabulary.excerpt: He says that the supreme leader and those around him
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia mengatakan Pemimpin Tertinggi dan orang-orang di sekitarnya

vocabulary.level: Latar Belakang Nama Khusus · Tingkat Tinggi
vocabulary.en: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps
vocabulary.excerpt: the IRGC, the -- the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Korps Garda Revolusi Islam Iran -- Garda Revolusi semi-militer.

vocabulary.en: able to see things clearly; realistic
vocabulary.excerpt: he needs to be a little more clear-eyed and a little more patient.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia perlu lebih berpikiran jernih dan lebih sabar.

vocabulary.cn: jauh di depan (sering berarti tidak realistis)
vocabulary.en: to be far in advance of something, often unrealistically
vocabulary.excerpt: his rhetoric right now is getting so far ahead of where the paper is
vocabulary.exampleCn: Retorikanya sekarang jauh di depan konten dokumen yang sebenarnya.

vocabulary.en: to make someone feel ashamed or foolish
vocabulary.excerpt: he runs the risk of not just humiliating the United States
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia mengambil risiko tidak hanya mempermalukan Amerika

vocabulary.en: to weaken or damage gradually
vocabulary.excerpt: undermining our relationships across the region.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Merusak hubungan kita di seluruh wilayah.

vocabulary.cn: tembok (metafora Trump untuk penghalang senjata nuklir)
vocabulary.en: a barrier; metaphor for preventing nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: what he is producing is going to be a wall preventing a nuclear weapon
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apa yang dia bangun akan menjadi tembok untuk mencegah senjata nuklir.

vocabulary.cn: jalan menuju senjata nuklir (merujuk pada perjanjian yang justru menyebabkan senjata nuklir)
vocabulary.en: a path that leads to acquiring nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: what he calls a road to a nuclear weapon
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apa yang dia sebut sebagai jalan menuju senjata nuklir

vocabulary.en: feeling a sense of hopelessness; extremely eager
vocabulary.excerpt: He's desperate to get this thing over
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia sangat menginginkan untuk mengakhiri ini.

vocabulary.en: feeling worried or nervous
vocabulary.excerpt: the American people are anxious about this war.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Rakyat Amerika merasa cemas terhadap perang ini.

vocabulary.en: a strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman
vocabulary.excerpt: It's hard to have a wall in the Strait of Hormuz
vocabulary.exampleCn: Sulit membangun tembok di Selat Hormuz.

vocabulary.en: a fee for using a road or waterway
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump is saying there will not be a toll on the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Trump mengatakan tidak akan ada biaya tol di Selat Hormuz.

vocabulary.en: U.S. dollar bills (green in color)
vocabulary.excerpt: He paid 1.7 billion from an airplane, all green cash.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dia membayar 1,7 miliar dolar dari pesawat, semuanya tunai.

sentences.en: And if I didn't do that -- Howard knows better than anybody -- if I didn't terminate that deal, I believe that they would have -- Israel wouldn't be here right now.
sentences.cn: Jika saya tidak melakukan itu -- Howard tahu lebih baik dari siapa pun -- jika saya tidak menghentikan perjanjian itu, saya yakin mereka sudah -- Israel sekarang tidak akan ada.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama adalah I believe that..., mengandung dua klausa if bersyarat yang paralel, diselingi dengan Howard knows better than anybody, dan klausa objek dari kalimat utama mengandung ekspresi elipsis yang disisipkan dengan tanda pisah.
sentences.analysis: 1. Sisipan: Howard knows better than anybody memotong klausa bersyarat, meningkatkan daya persuasi. 2. Elipsis: they would have diikuti dengan penghilangan done something, umum dalam bahasa lisan. 3. Suasana tidak nyata: if I didn't...would have...menunjukkan kebalikan dari fakta masa lalu.

sentences.en: And the second one was what we did with respect to the nuclear dust, as I call it.
sentences.cn: Hal kedua adalah tindakan yang kami ambil terhadap apa yang disebut 'debu nuklir'.
sentences.structure: Struktur subjek-kata kerja-pelengkap, pelengkap adalah klausa nominal yang diawali what, dan as I call it adalah sisipan.
sentences.analysis: 1. with respect to：关于，新闻常用。2. as I call it：插入语，表示自创术语。3. nuclear dust：比喻说法，指核材料残留。

sentences.en: And if they had a nuclear weapon, they would have used it on Israel.
sentences.cn: 如果他们当时拥有核武器，他们就会对以色列使用。
sentences.structure: if引导的虚拟条件句，主句为would have used。
sentences.analysis: 1. 虚拟语气：与过去事实相反，表示假设。2. used it on：对……使用，介词on表示目标。

sentences.en: And actually, that was a mistake for them, because it brought us these countries, in particular, the five countries -- it brought us.
sentences.cn: 实际上，这对他们来说是个错误，因为这让我们得到了这些国家，特别是那五个国家 -- -- 让我们得到了。
sentences.structure: 主句为that was a mistake，because引导原因状语从句，in particular为插入语，破折号后重复强调。
sentences.analysis: 1. in particular：特别是，用于列举。2. 破折号重复：it brought us 重复强调结果。3. 口语化表达：逻辑跳跃，需结合上下文理解。

sentences.en: But you wouldn't have -- if it weren't for the United States of America -- with me, because Obama was the opposite -- Israel would not exist right now.
sentences.cn: 但是你们就不会 -- -- 如果没有美利坚合众国 -- -- 有我，因为奥巴马正好相反 -- -- 以色列现在就不会存在。
sentences.structure: 主句为you wouldn't have...，中间插入if it weren't for...和because从句，破折号分隔。
sentences.analysis: 1. if it weren't for：要不是，虚拟语气。2. 插入语：with me 补充说明。3. 口语省略：you wouldn't have 后省略了宾语。

sentences.en: Israel would have been blown off the face of the Earth, 100 percent.
sentences.cn: 以色列早就被从地球上抹去了，百分之百。
sentences.structure: 简单句，would have been blown 为被动语态的虚拟语气。
sentences.analysis: 1. blown off the face of the Earth：夸张表达，意为彻底摧毁。2. 100 percent：口语强调，相当于absolutely。

sentences.en: He spent the bulk of his time talking about Israel, reacting to Bibi Netanyahu and his approach to the region, especially with his handling of the war in -- in Southern Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 他把大部分时间花在谈论以色列上，回应内塔尼亚胡及其对该地区的做法，特别是他处理 -- -- 处理南黎巴嫩战争的方式。
sentences.structure: 主句为He spent the bulk of his time talking about...，reacting to...为伴随状语，especially with...进一步说明。
sentences.analysis: 1. the bulk of：大部分，新闻常用。2. reacting to：回应，表示评论。3. handling of：处理方式。4. 重复in：口语修正。

sentences.en: There was a moment where he spoke quite a bit about Iran itself, talking about how the first tier of leadership was gone after strikes, and then the second tier of leadership was gone after U.S. strikes.
sentences.cn: 有一个时刻他相当多地谈论伊朗本身，谈到第一层领导层在打击后消失，然后第二层领导层在美国打击后消失。
sentences.structure: 主句为There was a moment where...，where引导定语从句，talking about...为伴随状语，how引导宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. a moment where：……的时刻。2. quite a bit：相当多。3. first/second tier of leadership：第一/二层领导层，新闻术语。4. gone after：在……之后消失。

sentences.en: Clearly, he spent most of the time obsessing over how he did a better deal than Obama, when we don't even have a deal right now on the nuclear program.
sentences.cn: 显然，他大部分时间都在纠结他如何比奥巴马做得更好，而我们现在甚至还没有一个关于核计划的协议。
sentences.structure: 主句为he spent most of the time obsessing over...，how引导宾语从句，when引导对比状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. obsessing over：纠结于，沉迷于。2. do a better deal than：比……做得更好。3. when：此处表对比，意为"而"。

sentences.en: And I think that's the key thing to understand, is that where President Trump is thinking things are is not actually where they are when it comes to the Iranian leadership.
sentences.cn: 我认为关键要理解的是，特朗普总统认为的情况实际上并非伊朗领导层的真实情况。
sentences.structure: 主句为I think that's the key thing to understand，后接is that表语从句，其中包含where引导的主语从句和表语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. the key thing to understand：关键要理解的事。2. where...is not where...：……的地方不是……的地方。3. when it comes to：当谈到，关于。

sentences.en: And his rhetoric right now is getting so far ahead of where the paper is that he runs the risk of not just humiliating the United States but also undermining our relationships across the region.
sentences.cn: 他现在的言论远远超出了文件的实际内容，以至于他不仅冒着羞辱美国的风险，还冒着破坏我们与整个地区关系的风险。
sentences.structure: 主句为his rhetoric...is getting so far ahead of...that...，so...that...结果状语从句，not just...but also...并列结构。
sentences.analysis: 1. get ahead of：超前于。2. where the paper is：文件的实际状态。3. run the risk of：冒……风险。4. humiliating/undermining：羞辱/破坏，动词并列。

sentences.en: We may very well find ourselves with no new agreement.
sentences.cn: 我们很可能发现自己没有新协议。
sentences.structure: 简单句，may very well表示可能性很大。
sentences.analysis: 1. may very well：很可能，加强语气。2. find ourselves with：发现自己处于……状态。

sentences.en: But the president saying he has an agreement, and then the region, of course, ends up in this sort of uncertainty.
sentences.cn: 但总统说他有一个协议，然后该地区当然最终陷入这种不确定性。
sentences.structure: 并列句，前半句为the president saying...（独立主格或口语省略），后半句为the region ends up...。
sentences.analysis: 1. saying：现在分词作伴随，口语中常见。2. of course：插入语。3. end up in：最终陷入。

sentences.en: One of the things we heard him say today and has said in the past is that what he is producing is going to be a wall preventing a nuclear weapon versus what he calls a road to a nuclear weapon, which is how he characterizes the Obama administration agreements.
sentences.cn: 我们今天听到他说的以及他过去说过的一件事是，他正在打造的将是一堵防止核武器的墙，而他称之为通往核武器的路，这是他形容奥巴马政府协议的方式。
sentences.structure: 主句为One of the things...is that...，that引导表语从句，其中包含what主语从句和which非限制性定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. we heard him say：省略that的定语从句。2. a wall preventing...versus a road to...：对比结构。3. characterize...as：将……描述为。

sentences.en: No American wants Iran to have a nuclear weapon, but they also don't want $4 a gallon of gas.
sentences.cn: 没有美国人希望伊朗拥有核武器，但他们也不想要每加仑4美元的汽油。
sentences.structure: 并列句，but连接两个否定句。
sentences.analysis: 1. No American：全部否定。2. $4 a gallon of gas：每加仑4美元汽油，新闻常用价格表达。

sentences.en: And we all know Donald Trump, and I even give Donald Trump some credit on his politics, like he was to come out and say, I got this done, I fixed it.
sentences.cn: 我们都了解唐纳德·特朗普，我甚至在他的政治上给他一些肯定，比如他会站出来说，我搞定了，我解决了。
sentences.structure: 并列句，and连接两个分句，like引导举例。
sentences.analysis: 1. give credit on：在……方面给予肯定。2. come out and say：公开说。3. got this done/fixed it：口语化表达，意为完成/解决。

sentences.en: And y'all know I'm the wall guy.
sentences.cn: 你们都知道我是那个筑墙的人。
sentences.structure: 简单句，y'all为you all缩写。
sentences.analysis: 1. y'all：美国南方口语，你们。2. the wall guy：指特朗普标志性的边境墙政策。

sentences.en: We appreciate the relationship we've had over this short period of time with Iran.
sentences.cn: 我们感谢在这短暂时间内与伊朗建立的关系。
sentences.structure: 主句为We appreciate the relationship，后接省略that的定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. appreciate：menghargai, sering digunakan dalam berita. 2. over this short period of time：dalam waktu singkat ini.

sentences.en: But the one thing that's happening that's of note, frankly, the only thing that really matters in it, is Iran will never have a nuclear weapon.
sentences.cn: Tetapi satu hal yang patut diperhatikan yang sedang terjadi, dan terus terang satu-satunya hal yang benar-benar penting, adalah bahwa Iran tidak akan pernah memiliki senjata nuklir.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama adalah the one thing...is..., that's happening dan that's of note adalah klausa relatif, frankly adalah sisipan, the only thing... adalah aposisi.
sentences.analysis: 1. of note：patut diperhatikan. 2. frankly：terus terang, sisipan. 3. that really matters：yang benar-benar penting.

sentences.en: We want to know that we are actually standing in the gap and making sure that we are not going to be -- be abused and traumatized by the Iranian patriarchy.
sentences.cn: Kami ingin tahu bahwa kami benar-benar mengisi kekosongan, memastikan bahwa kami tidak -- -- tidak dianiaya dan trauma oleh patriarki Iran.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama adalah We want to know that..., that memimpin klausa objek, dalam klausa tersebut and menghubungkan dua predikat sejajar, setelah tanda pisah diulang be.
sentences.analysis: 1. stand in the gap：mengisi kekosongan, metafora perlindungan. 2. making sure that：memastikan bahwa. 3. be abused and traumatized：kalimat pasif, dianiaya dan trauma. 4. patriarchy：patriarki.

topics.title: Perbandingan Perjanjian Nuklir Iran Trump vs Obama
topics.content: Pada KTT G7, Trump berulang kali mengkritik Perjanjian Nuklir Iran (JCPOA) yang ditandatangani pemerintah Obama pada 2015, menyebutnya membayar uang tunai besar (1,7 miliar dolar) kepada Iran, dan menuduh perjanjian itu memberi Iran jalan menuju senjata nuklir. Ia mengklaim bahwa ia mengakhiri perjanjian dan menghancurkan fasilitas nuklir, mencegah Iran memiliki nuklir. Namun, para ahli menunjukkan bahwa Trump saat ini belum mencapai kesepakatan baru, dan pernyataannya tidak sesuai dengan kenyataan.
topics.keywords: JCPOA · nuclear deal · terminate · green cash

topics.title: Situasi Kepemimpinan Iran Saat Ini dan Peran IRGC
topics.content: Trump mengklaim bahwa eselon pertama dan kedua kepemimpinan Iran telah dieliminasi, dan kepemimpinan saat ini rasional. Namun, pakar Joel Rubin menunjukkan bahwa Pemimpin Tertinggi Iran (Supreme Leader) dan Garda Revolusi Islam (IRGC) masih memegang kekuasaan nyata, dan kepemimpinan tidak berubah secara fundamental. IRGC adalah pasukan paramiliter Iran yang memiliki pengaruh besar terhadap program nuklir dan masalah keamanan.
topics.keywords: Supreme Leader · IRGC · first tier · second tier

topics.title: Kontroversi Biaya Lintas Selat Hormuz
topics.content: Trump mengatakan tidak akan mengizinkan Iran memungut biaya lintas (toll) di Selat Hormuz (Strait of Hormuz), sementara pihak Iran mengisyaratkan kemungkinan mengenakan biaya. Selat Hormuz adalah jalur transportasi minyak terpenting di dunia, sekitar 20% minyak diangkut melaluinya. Kontroversi ini menyangkut kebebasan navigasi, keamanan energi, dan ketegangan regional.
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · toll · oil transit · freedom of navigation

topics.title: Keraguan Internal Partai Republik terhadap Nota Kesepahaman Iran
topics.content: Meskipun Trump mengumumkan telah mencapai Nota Kesepahaman (Memorandum of Agreement, MOA) dengan Iran, Senator Republik James Lankford dan Lindsey Graham meminta agar rincian perjanjian dipublikasikan. Komentator konservatif Mark Levin juga mengkritik ketidaktransparanan perjanjian. Para ahli menunjukkan bahwa kurangnya rincian dapat merusak kredibilitas AS dan hubungan dengan sekutu regional.
topics.keywords: Memorandum of Agreement · transparency · Republican · Mark Levin

quiz.question: Dalam kalimat asli 'if I didn't terminate that deal', 'terminate' paling mendekati arti?
quiz.explanation: terminate berarti 'mengakhiri', Trump merujuk pada penghentian perjanjian nuklir Iran era Obama.

quiz.question: 原文 'the IRGC, the -- the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard' 中，'IRGC' 指什么？
options: B. 伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队
quiz.explanation: IRGC 是 Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps 的缩写，即伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队，是伊朗重要的军事力量。

quiz.question: 原文 'Trump is saying there will not be a toll on the Strait of Hormuz' 中，'toll' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: toll 在此处指 '通行费'，特朗普反对伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡收取费用。

quiz.question: 根据对话，特朗普对伊朗核协议的主要批评是什么？
options: B. 美国支付了太多现金
C. 协议没有涉及导弹
quiz.explanation: 特朗普批评奥巴马政府支付了17亿美元现金（green cash），并称该协议是通往核武器之路。

quiz.question: 专家Joel Rubin认为特朗普对伊朗领导层的看法存在什么问题？
options: A. 特朗普高估了伊朗的威胁
B. 特朗普低估了IRGC的持续控制
C. 特朗普忽略了以色列的角色
quiz.explanation: Rubin指出，尽管特朗普声称领导层已改变，但IRGC仍控制伊朗，实际领导层未变。

quiz.question: Apa kekhawatiran utama Partai Republik terhadap nota kesepakatan Iran?
quiz.explanation: Beberapa anggota Partai Republik seperti Lankford, Graham, dan Levin meminta agar rincian kesepakatan dipublikasikan, mengkritik ketidaktransparannya.
